\documentclass{article}
\title{Minimal Smoke Test}
\author{Test Author}
\date{2026-05-04}

\begin{document}
\maketitle

\section{Introduction}
This is the smallest viable LaTeX project — one main file, no bibliography,
no figures, no markup. It exists to smoke-test the entire conversion
pipeline end-to-end. If \texttt{latex2arxiv} fails on this, something is
deeply broken.

\section{Body}
The cleaning pipeline strips comments, ensures pdfLaTeX output mode by
prepending the appropriate directive to the main file, and packages
everything into an arXiv-ready zip. This document tests that the
round-trip leaves the source readable and pdfLaTeX still compiles.

\end{document}
